rules.name: Blockout before detail
rules.description: Build the 2D plan and a low-detail blockout before placing detailed art. Detailing early locks in decisions that gameplay testing will overturn.
rules.prompt_hint: Prefer rough blockout-level intent over detail-heavy descriptions.

rules.name: Identify gameplay locations first
rules.description: Locate gameplay anchors (objectives, vistas, encounters) before laying roads and districts. The environment funnels the player toward gameplay.
rules.prompt_hint: List the gameplay anchors first; the environment radiates outward from them.

rules.name: Every major district must connect to the road graph
rules.description: A district without an incoming road is unreachable unless the design intentionally hides it as a discovery target.
rules.validator_hint: district has at least one road within its bbox or a road point inside its polygon
rules.prompt_hint: Each district has at least one road tying it into the rest of the world.

rules.name: Stealth profiles need alternate routes
rules.description: Districts whose gameplay profile includes stealth should have ≥ 2 ways in or out.
rules.prompt_hint: Stealth zones offer multiple alternative entries (back doors, vents, alleys).

rules.name: Each major district carries a landmark
rules.description: Landmarks reduce confusion, create scale, and reward exploration. Districts marked as focal/objective/landmark/hub should have at least one.
rules.validator_hint: every district with focal/hub/objective/landmark role has ≥ 1 Landmark inside its bbox
rules.prompt_hint: Highlight a single visible landmark per major district.

rules.name: Landmarks need payoff
rules.description: If a landmark is reachable it should reward the player with a vista, an objective, loot, story, or a unique scene slot — not nothing.
rules.prompt_hint: When proposing a landmark, also name the payoff (vista / objective / loot / story).

rules.name: Use arterial → collector → local hierarchy
rules.description: City templates should distribute road types so player vehicles can find arterials and on-foot routes can find narrow streets.
rules.prompt_hint: Plan arterial, collector, and local roads — not one road type everywhere.

rules.name: Driving profile demands arterial width
rules.description: Districts with the driving profile need arterials ≥ the metrics.road.arterial_width_m floor (default 14m).
rules.validator_hint: driving districts: every arterial road has width_m >= metrics.road.arterial_width_m
rules.prompt_hint: Driving zones use wide arterials, not narrow alleys.

rules.name: Shooter encounters need cover intervals
rules.description: Scenes with the shooter profile need cover at least every metrics.shooter.cover_interval_m metres.
rules.validator_hint: shooter zones: ≥ 1 cover marker or cover-tagged object per cover_interval_m sqm
rules.prompt_hint: Place cover roughly every 4-6m for shooter encounters.

rules.name: Preview labels should not all overlap
rules.description: If the preview suppresses many overlapping labels the world is probably too dense to read at a glance. Increase district size or thin out POIs.
rules.validator_hint: if preview reports >5 dropped labels, surface a warning
rules.prompt_hint: Avoid placing many tiny POIs in the same region — readability suffers.

rules.name: Districts need a differentiating feature
rules.description: Each district must carry a type / theme / gameplay tag so it reads as distinct.
rules.validator_hint: district.tags or theme or distinct district_type must be set
rules.prompt_hint: Give every district a theme, role, or unique gameplay tag.

rules.name: Define the arena's boundaries
rules.description: Worldbuilding should establish where the world starts and ends, the dominant conflict, and the factions in tension.
rules.prompt_hint: State the world's boundaries, central conflict, and the factions involved.

rules.name: Lead the eye toward the next goal
rules.description: Roads, shadows, light contrast, and silhouettes should point toward the next gameplay focal point.
rules.prompt_hint: Use leading lines (a road, a shadow, a row of lanterns) toward each major landmark.

rules.name: Breadcrumbs create organic paths
rules.description: Small repeated cues (lanterns, footprints, flowers) imply a path without UI markers.
rules.prompt_hint: Use breadcrumbs of small props to suggest a route.

rules.name: Hide some map content
rules.description: Avoid filling the entire world with markers at start — fog of war / partial visibility supports curiosity. Balance against frustration.
rules.prompt_hint: Reveal half the map at a time; the rest is for discovery.

rules.name: Buildings live inside parcels
rules.description: Generated buildings must lie inside their declared parcel polygon (set-back permitted).
rules.prompt_hint: Building footprints sit inside their parcels.

rules.name: Expect to discard early blockouts
rules.description: First passes are wrong on purpose. Playtest and iterate before detailing.
rules.prompt_hint: Treat the first pass as throwaway; do not over-commit detail.
